name: I. M. Pei
reason: for his mastery of light, geometry, and materials, exemplified by the Louvre Pyramid and Bank of China Tower

name: Frank Gehry
reason: for his sculptural, deconstructivist designs that transformed the landscape of contemporary architecture

name: Tadao Ando
reason: for his minimalist concrete architecture that masterfully integrates light and nature

name: Renzo Piano
reason: for his poetic, light-filled buildings including the Centre Pompidou and the Shard

name: Norman Foster
reason: for his high-tech architecture blending engineering innovation with sustainable design

name: Rem Koolhaas
reason: for his visionary architectural theories and innovative buildings that challenge conventions

name: Zaha Hadid
reason: for her bold, futuristic forms and being the first woman to receive the Pritzker Prize

name: Kazuyo Sejima / Ryue Nishizawa
reason: for their transparent,轻盈 architecture that blurs boundaries between interior and exterior

name: Shigeru Ban
reason: for his innovative use of paper and cardboard in disaster relief housing and sustainable design

name: Alejandro Aravena
reason: for his socially engaged architecture addressing housing crises through participatory design

name: David Chipperfield
reason: for his restrained, timeless architecture that respects history while embracing the contemporary

name: Tiantian Xu
reason: for her architecture that transformed villages throughout China economically, socially, and culturally
